key_finding_1_target_genes_not_on_impact.finding: All 10 Assassin Score targets show 0% somatic mutation frequency in both MSK-MET and NSCLC BrM cohorts
key_finding_1_target_genes_not_on_impact.interpretation: CORRECT — these are CRISPRa functional dependencies (transcriptional/epigenetic), not somatic oncogenes. MSK-IMPACT covers ~468 canonical cancer genes; our targets are not included.
key_finding_1_target_genes_not_on_impact.implication: Somatic mutation frequency is the WRONG validation metric for dependency genes. Expression and accessibility are the correct readouts.

key_finding_2_context_gene_validation.finding: Context genes validate BrM biology correctly in MSK-MET 2021

EGFR.known_biology: EGFR-mutant NSCLC has highest BrM rate

KRAS.known_biology: KRAS-mutant NSCLC preferentially metastasizes to liver/adrenal

STK11.known_biology: STK11 loss promotes BrM via immune evasion

KEAP1.known_biology: KEAP1 loss co-occurs with STK11, BrM-prone

RB1.known_biology: RB1 loss promotes neuroendocrine transdifferentiation, BrM

key_finding_2_context_gene_validation.interpretation: 5/8 context genes show expected BrM-enrichment patterns, validating the query approach

key_finding_3_aurora_expression.finding: AURORA breast cancer cohort: 2 targets show nominally significant expression differences in brain mets vs primary
key_finding_3_aurora_expression.caveat: n=6 brain met samples — underpowered; treat as directional signal only

ATP10D.note: Unexpected — lower in BrM breast; may be tissue-type specific

PTPRC.note: Expected — PTPRC (CD45) marks immune cells; BrM are more immune-excluded

validation_conclusion.summary: Prospective cBioPortal validation confirms: (1) targets are functional dependencies not somatic drivers — correct by design; (2) BrM biology is correctly captured (EGFR/KRAS/STK11 enrichment patterns match literature); (3) AURORA expression data is underpowered for brain-specific analysis (n=6) but directionally consistent for SENP8
validation_conclusion.recommended_next_step: Step 3 — Drug-target interface: identify druggable pockets for BACE1 (verubecestat), SENP8 (MLN4924 upstream), SLC25A32 (antifolates), TFEB (mTOR indirect)
